description: Canonical registry of all breakaway events. A breakaway occurs when a mirror or slice intentionally or unintentionally diverges from its source system to the degree that the reflection relationship is no longer maintainable. Breakaway is a terminal drift condition that requires explicit freeze, remediation, or retirement.

record_schema.mirror_id: string — Reference to the mirror in breakaway
record_schema.slice_id: string|null — Reference to the specific slice, if slice-level breakaway
record_schema.detected_at: datetime — ISO 8601 timestamp of breakaway detection
record_schema.detected_by: string — System or operator that detected the breakaway
record_schema.summary: string — Human-readable summary of the breakaway condition
record_schema.drift_ids: array — References to drift events that contributed to the breakaway
record_schema.impact_assessment: object — Structured impact assessment including affected_systems, data_integrity_risk, and operational_risk
record_schema.resolution_path: string|null — One of valid_resolution_paths, set during review
record_schema.freeze_record: object|null — Details of freeze action if applied, including frozen_at, frozen_by, freeze_scope
record_schema.remediation_record: object|null — Details of remediation if attempted, including plan, started_at, completed_at
record_schema.promotion_record: object|null — Details of promotion to core if applicable
record_schema.retirement_record: object|null — Details of retirement if applicable
record_schema.review_notes: array — Timestamped review notes from reviewers
